😳OMG Dora did a full blown magic mushroom trip in the middle of the movie (or it could have been the Mexican hallucinogenic plant “Peyote”). The audience is roaring with laughter but they don’t know that when them big ass flowers let off that gas and they started hallucinating and life became a fun cartoon with no worries or problems she was on a magic mushroom trip. After the trip she came back to reality and was contemplating over the fire thinking about how life is nothing but a great adventure and she will make it through bc it’s all a dream (an illusion). Am I the only one that caught that trip they cleverly slid in the movie? This is why I love kid films they be deep when you really paying attention. 
The fact that she is a teenager that can’t fit in bc she was raised in the Jungle as a scavenger hunter is the perfect storyline for all the kids that grew up with Dora. This movie was very good, Dora was so cute, fun, always positive, smart, and she had all the faith in the world. She would make a great best friend. Maybe we should homeschool our kids in the jungle? 
😻😻😻Overall very funny adventurous family movie, everyone will enjoy. 
Rating: 8.5 ⭐️

Ok this actually looks like a fun film, more than I thought it would. But now this has me thinking about the cartoon series, and now that I’m older there’s one thing I notice that makes me really wonder bc it’s never explained:
Why the hell do Dora’s parents let their six year old run around in a jungle/village where everything is BROKEN??? Like almost every episode some obstacle/thing in general is broken and it’s a danger to everyone around it. There were more broken bridges than I can count, lots of broken cars/trucks, a sailboat with holes in the bottom, etc. I think in one episode there was even a train with a broken steering wheel and it was up to them to stop the train from driving off a cliff XD. So basically every ep is like this: 

“OH NO! The bridge is broken and this lake is full of crocodiles! I guess we have to climb that tree and swing over the crocodiles with this vine!”
“UH OH! The steering wheel on the train is broken and we’re headed straight for a cliff! Looks like we have to get out and jump in front of the train so it sees us and stops!”
“AAH! That baby stroller broke and now the babies are rolling down a cliff towards that volcano! Let’s run up to the volcano with umbrellas to stop the lava from hitting us so we can catch the babies!!!”
